key_insight: PG-KINN pairs a KAN trial network with a Petrov-Galerkin weak form, improving PDE learning by reducing derivative burden and avoiding limitations of strong-form and energy-form physics-informed methods.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more robust and interpretable AI solver for forward and inverse solid-mechanics PDEs, including nonlinear, non-self-adjoint, and heterogeneous material problems.
method: KAN is used as the trial space with learnable spline activations.
An independent compactly supported piecewise-polynomial test space forms a Petrov-Galerkin weak residual.
Gauss-Legendre quadrature and integration by parts reduce differentiation order and improve conditioning.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent outperformance over MLP baselines and state-of-the-art KAN variants across benchmarks including crack singularities, stress concentration, hyperelasticity, inverse identification, and complex geometries.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful weak-form implementation, especially test-space design, quadrature accuracy, and element-wise assembly for complex domains.
paper_title: PG-KINN: A Physics-Informed Petrov-Galerkin Kolmogorov-Arnold Network for Solving Forward and Inverse PDEs
